steps.title: Find the linear function
steps.content:
Using the table points $(-4,0)$ and $(-\frac{19}{5},1)$, the slope is
$$m=\frac{1-0}{-\frac{19}{5}-(-4)}=\frac{1}{\frac{1}{5}}=5$$
Substitute $(-4,0)$ into $f(x)=5x+b$:
$$0=5(-4)+b$$
$$b=20$$

steps.title: Apply the function shift
steps.content:
So the function from the table is $f(x)=5x+20$. Since $h(x)=f(x)-13$, subtract $13$ from the constant term:
$$h(x)=(5x+20)-13=5x+7$$
The answer is B.
